verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A is a canonically cytoplasmic heat shock protein that lacks intrinsic membrane anchoring domains (no TM, no GPI, no secretory signal). However, extensive literature documents that under stress conditions (heat shock recovery) and prominently in tumor cells, HSPA1A translocates to the plasma membrane and reaches the outer leaflet via interaction with phosphatidylserine (PS) and other negatively charged phospholipids (PI(4)P, PI(3)P). Cell surface biotinylation and flow cytometry confirm outer-leaflet accessibility under these non-baseline conditions. Membrane-bound HSPA1A exhibits biological functions including immune activation, endocytosis mediation, and tumor cell survival signaling. This is cell-state-induced surface display driven by lipid-interaction—not constitutive or baseline accessible.
